_doc: CURRENCY layer of the close-out gate — fingerprints for NARRATIVE result references (per the 2026-06-21 reconciled design: generate-what's-mechanical via tools/render_state.py + docs/program_state.json; FINGERPRINT-what's-narrative here; warn on unregistered). Each key maps to a canonical SOURCE (file, optional anchored span); the gate hashes it (stamped ⟨KEY@hex⟩ markers stripped first) and requires '<KEY>@<8hex>' wherever the key is referenced. Source change -> hash change -> dependents read STALE. Cmds: `closeout_check.py <D-ID>` (presence+currency, active close-out); `--currency <KEY>`; `--audit` (all); `--fp <KEY>`. Keys absent here = presence-only.
_not_fingerprinted: STRUCTURED program STATUS (F1 scorecard, current-position/next-actions, governance CP1-G3 status, §0.3/§10/§12 tables, dashboards) is NOT fingerprinted — it is GENERATED from docs/program_state.json via tools/render_state.py into <!-- BEGIN GENERATED:KEY --> blocks, auto-synced (the real auto-update). Stable decision-ledger D-IDs (D-SCOPE-1, D-S242/S243 recipe locks) are presence-only.

D-D1-1.what: D1 capacity law: drift = per-relation concentration, not global edge-count

D-B1-2.what: D1 concentration law model-size term (REPLICATES on 7B; model-general)

D-A2-1.what: in-solve relation-balanced sentinels halve but don't arrest incremental corruption

D-A2b-1.what: K_S staleness RULED OUT (per-edit refresh doesn't reduce cross-entity corruption)

D-B3-1.what: A1-clean batch store survives real Q4_K_M (edits 100% vs native 97.4%)

D-E1-1.what: CPU deployment loop via llama.cpp+Q4_K_M; LARQL gguf-to-vindex falsified for Qwen2.5

D-B1-1.what: A1 batch-clean does NOT fully replicate at 7B (100->91.7%); scopes A1 to 3B/N<=100

D-C2-1.what: relation-keying pruned; same-relation key collinearity U-shaped, min L8-12

D-G6-1.what: first empirical falsifier: cross-entity read corruption scales with N (100->42%)
